free_text: Multistep direct analysis of (P,a) reactions to the
continuum.
The explanation of some details.
Details of
experiment.
Last checking has been done.
Enriched  self-supporting targets, Zr-90-97.65%,
approx. 700 mug/cm**2, were used.
The Hauser-Feshbach and Feshbach-Kerman-Koonin
theories.
Alpha spectrum was measured.
A telescope of two SSD-detectors, consisting of
a Delta E , transparent (50-mum,100 mm**2), an E-
detector (1500-mum,100 mm**2), backed by a 1000-mum,
100 mm**2 veto counter to reject lighter particles.
The Milan university AVF cyclotron.
.By CAJAD.
Figure2a
.Data-point reader uncertainty.
.Data-point reader uncertainty.
The absolute cross sections were estimated
taking into account target thickness, solid angle,
isotopic enrichment and collected charge with an
overall error of 20% approximately.
- By authors. To separate the compound nucleus and
multistep direct components the cross sections at
complementary angles were subtracted in the C.-M.
System.
- By compiler: the authors presented cross sections as
a function of the sum of kinetic energies of alpha -
particle and yttrium-87. The energies were recalculated
to alpha-particle energy. The authors' data are saved
in MISC-COL.
The sum of kinetic energies of alpha-particle
and yttrium-87
